title: Candidate pairwise Poly-encoder scorer

queries: Poly-encoders multi-sentence scoring efficient candidate scoring
candidate ranking poly encoder context codes response selection

papers.title: Poly-encoders: Architectures and Pre-training Strategies for Fast and Accurate Multi-sentence Scoring
papers.claim: Poly-encoders use a small fixed set of learned context codes to produce multiple context vectors, then let each candidate attend over those codes for efficient candidate-aware scoring.
papers.relevance: This matches the Stage 3 need for a richer sample-candidate interaction than single-vector pooling while avoiding exhaustive per-candidate LLM rollout and the slow shared-prefix recursion path.

evidence.why_now: EXP093 established that candidate-conditioned pairwise interaction carries real signal. EXP099/EXP108 showed that candidate-token evidence is the strongest salt-side signal but exhaustive or recursive token scoring is not the right scalable endpoint. EXP156/EXP159 exhausted token-stat and residual near-neighbors, EXP162 showed direct hard-negative pressure is directionally useful but insufficient, EXP168 showed naive hidden-token late interaction is scientifically insufficient, and the EXP171 audit showed the batched shared-prefix rewrite is not score-exact. A Poly-encoder scorer is therefore the next different information path: fixed-size learned context codes preserve multi-vector sample structure and candidate-conditioned scoring without O(L*C) LLM rollout.

hypothesis: A Poly-encoder autocomplete scorer can recover more of the candidate-token discrimination lost by single-vector pairwise and naive MaxSim late interaction by extracting a small learned set of prompt context vectors, while keeping inference complexity at O(L*m + C*m) for fixed m and preserving a Stage4-compatible sample-candidate ranking interface.
notes: Keep Stage 3 finetune-only; do not use --pretrain.
This is not a pooled-label/token-stat near variant: it uses fixed learned context codes over prompt token states and candidate-conditioned attention over those codes.
Do not hand-scan autocomplete_poly_codes; expose the knob for future Optuna only if the structural family clears screening.
This candidate must pass py_compile, git diff --check, JSON validation, and a remote item-incoterms shape/throughput sanity check before launch.
Throughput gate: do not launch EXP174/175/176 if item-incoterms shows a long-tail signature comparable to the old shared-prefix roughly 1.25 train steps/sec path.
Exclude GPU0 for this candidate only because recent EXP162 evidence showed a pre-eval SIGTERM when user-churn used GPU0 as local rank 0; global pipeline_config keeps gpu_exclude empty.

Static validation on 2026-05-10: python -m py_compile passed for tune_hyperparameters.py, main.py, model.py, stage3_research.py, and stage3_orchestrator.py; git diff --check passed; candidate JSON validated.
Remote item-incoterms sanity check completed on 2026-05-10: 64-step single-GPU run on lab25211 GPU7 entered training, completed cleanly, and did not use --pretrain.

Sanity throughput evidence: after warmup the single-GPU training loop reached roughly 3.5-3.7 train steps/sec with batch_size=4, i.e. about 14.0-14.8 train items/sec. Validation used val_size=1 and ran around 6.7-6.8 eval items/sec. This clears the practical throughput gate relative to the old shared-prefix long-tail signature, though it is slower than late interaction and should be watched closely.

Sanity conclusion: launch is allowed once enough GPUs are idle for packed_batch_equivalent scheduling; at the 2026-05-10T10:20+08:00 check, lab25211 had no GPUs below the configured idle memory threshold, so the full bundle was not launched in this pass.

Finalized on 2026-05-10: EXP174 user-churn was better with roc_auc=0.6593831778701815, EXP175 user-ltv was neutral with mae=82.46907187100966 under the wide replay-aware band, and EXP176 item-incoterms was worse with mrr=0.6886574769631411 versus baseline 0.8147880873466811.

Throughput evidence from the completed bundle: EXP176 item-incoterms ran around 5.67 train steps/sec on two ranks with per-rank batch 2, i.e. about 22.7 effective train items/sec; final test progress was around 6.86 it/sec with val/test batch effectively 1. EXP174 user-churn finished with final test progress around 12.81 it/sec. The bundle failure is therefore scientific, not a practical-throughput failure.

Interpretation: the Poly-encoder scorer remained Stage4-compatible and computationally scalable, but the salt-side score surface still landed near the repeated 0.678576 collapse band rather than in the retune-plausible 0.75-ish region. Do not hand-scan autocomplete_poly_codes; treat this implementation path as failed unless a new paper- or ablation-backed mechanism changes the information path.
